items.q: How do you say 'I travel by train' in Spanish?
items.why: Students use por or con. Transport always uses EN: en coche, en avión, en autobús, en tren, en barco. Exception: a pie (on foot), a caballo (on horseback). Critical holiday/travel vocab.

items.q: How do you say 'I'm going to the beach' (talking to someone elsewhere) in Spanish?
items.why: Students confuse ir (to go) and venir (to come). IR = movement AWAY from speaker; VENIR = movement TOWARDS speaker. 'I'm going there' = voy; 'I'm coming here' = vengo. Critical for holidays/travel vocab.

items.q: What is the gender of 'el día' (day)?
items.why: Students rely on '-a = feminine'. Common exceptions: el día, el mapa, el problema, el sistema, el clima (many Greek-origin -ma words). These appear constantly so must be memorised.
The answer examiners want is: a pie. “en pie” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's los transportes públicos. “el coche privado” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many transport modes in: coche, autobús, tren, bicicleta?
items.why: The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
